prior_art.finding: Already tracks pipeline-level gaming signals across runs using an append-only GOVERNOR_LOG.md. The storage pattern (append-only, structured entries, never mutate) is the right model for waiver records.
prior_art.relevance: Reuse the same append-only log contract for waiver_log.json.

prior_art.finding: Every MMD_REPORT.json already has a pattern_checks array with {pattern_id, addressed, waiver_reason} per pattern match. This is the natural source of waiver events — we do not need a new input surface.
prior_art.relevance: WaiverTracker.record_waiver() can be called directly by s3_mmd after pattern_checks parsing. The data is already there.

prior_art.finding: Append-only, human+machine readable, structured JSON entries per run. Corrections are separate entries, not edits.

code_shapes: append-only log file with JSON entries
accuracy ratio computation over a filtered time window
query for records matching team + pattern_id + timestamp range

key_decisions.decision: Store waivers and incidents in a single append-only file (waiver_log.json) with an entry type field
key_decisions.rationale: Waivers and incidents are correlated events — they must be queried together. Separate files require joins. Single file with type: waiver | incident | correction is simpler and consistent with Governor Log pattern.

key_decisions.decision: accuracy() returns None when waiver count < MIN_SAMPLE (5)
key_decisions.rationale: A team with 2 waivers and 1 incident has a 50% accuracy score that looks meaningful but isn't. Returning None forces the caller to handle the insufficient-data case explicitly.

key_decisions.decision: Window-based incident matching (default 90 days) not lifetime
key_decisions.rationale: A waiver from 3 years ago should not count against a team for an incident today — the system and team have changed. The window is the claim horizon: 'this pattern will not cause an incident in the next N days.' If it does, the waiver was wrong.

key_decisions.decision: No integration with s3_mmd.py in this turn — WaiverTracker is a standalone library
key_decisions.rationale: MPP sub-check 6 integration is a separate change with its own gate chain. This turn builds and tests the core library only.
